headlines.title: Japan’s Nikkei Falls after Eight-Session Winning Streak
Top Japan Official Reaffirms Readiness to Bolster Yen
Asian shares are trading mixed amid caution about the war in Iran
Japan's Nikkei falls after eight-session winning streak
Private bank LGT sees AI and inflation as Japan tailwinds
Can words stop yen’s slide? Japan and US reaffirm readiness for market intervention
Japanese yen nears weakest level in 40 years; govt intervention in focus

marketTone.ko: 일본 증시는 8거래일 연속 상승세를 마감하며 하락했습니다. 엔화는 40년 만에 최약 수준에 근접하면서 일본과 미국 당국이 시장 개입 준비 태세를 재확인하는 등 엔화 약세가 주요 관심사로 부상했습니다. 아시아 증시는 이란 전쟁에 대한 경계심 속에 혼조세를 보였으며, 이러한 지정학적 우려가 전반적인 시장 분위기에 영향을 미쳤습니다.
marketTone.en: The Japanese Nikkei index fell today, breaking an eight-session winning streak. A primary concern is the Japanese yen, which is nearing a 40-year low, leading Japanese and US officials to reaffirm their readiness for market intervention. Broader Asian shares are trading mixed, reflecting caution over geopolitical developments, particularly the war in Iran. Today's market sentiment is largely shaped by yen weakness and global caution.
